message:
docs: add FCP Specification V3 — Asupersync-native re-foundation

Add the complete draft of Flywheel Connector Protocol Specification V3
(3353 lines), a ground-up rewrite that re-founds FCP as an
Asupersync-native secure connector operating model.

Key design decisions in V3:
- No backwards compatibility with FCP2/FCP2.5 — clean break
- Spec-first re-foundation: FCP_Specification_V3.md is the primary
  source of truth, reading as if the platform was always designed this way
- Asupersync-native execution semantics: zone binding, capability
  narrowing, provenance, approval scope, deadlines, budgets,
  cancellation, supervision, drain, quiescence, and failure escalation
  are first-class runtime semantics (Cx, Scope, Budget, Outcome, AppSpec)
- Connectors are supervised applications with explicit topology,
  lifecycle, state, and transport boundaries
- Mechanical security: zero-trust zones, capability tokens, provenance
  chains, audit receipts, and supply-chain evidence
- Mesh-native placement, mobility, and offline recovery
- Comprehensive conformance levels and verification model

Covers: execution model, authority/zones/provenance, durable object
model, host model, connector application model, control/data/evidence
planes, manifest/provisioning/isolation, placement/mobility/mesh,
registry/supply-chain, observability/explainability, and conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
